title: Tripleurospermum heterolepis
intro: Tripleurospermum heterolepis, also known as small-flowered meadow cress, is a plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its small white flowers and narrow leaves. The health benefits of Tripleurospermum heterolepis include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, reduce oxidative stress, and protect against cell damage. Bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Tripleurospermum heterolepis include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Tripleurospermum heterolepis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Tripleurospermum, which is a group of daisy-like plants. The genus Tripleurospermum is classified under the tribe Anthemideae, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The tribe Anthemideae is part of the subfamily Asteroideae, one of the largest and most widespread subfamilies of flowering plants. The subfamily Asteroideae is found in various parts of the world, from tropical regions to temperate and arctic areas.

side_effects: Tripleurospermum heterolepis, also known as the rough hawk's beard or rough false chamomile, is a plant that is often used in herbal remedies. However, like many plants, it may cause adverse reactions in some individuals. These side effects can include allergic reactions, such as skin irritation, and gastrointestinal issues, like nausea and vomiting. In rare cases, ingesting the plant can lead to more severe reactions, including diarrhea and abdominal pain.
